execution_details.stdout:
# Use public servers from the pool.ntp.org project.
# Please consider joining the pool (https://www.pool.ntp.org/join.html).
pool 2.rocky.pool.ntp.org iburst maxpoll 10

# Use NTP servers from DHCP.
sourcedir /run/chrony-dhcp

# Record the rate at which the system clock gains/losses time.
driftfile /var/lib/chrony/drift

# Allow the system clock to be stepped in the first three updates
# if its offset is larger than 1 second.
makestep 1.0 3

# Enable kernel synchronization of the real-time clock (RTC).
rtcsync

# Enable hardware timestamping on all interfaces that support it.
#hwtimestamp *

# Increase the minimum number of selectable sources required to adjust
# the system clock.
#minsources 2

# Allow NTP client access from local network.
#allow 192.168.0.0/16

# Serve time even if not synchronized to a time source.
#local stratum 10

# Require authentication (nts or key option) for all NTP sources.
#authselectmode require

# Specify file containing keys for NTP authentication.
keyfile /etc/chrony.keys

# Save NTS keys and cookies.
ntsdumpdir /var/lib/chrony

# Insert/delete leap seconds by slewing instead of stepping.
#leapsecmode slew

# Get TAI-UTC offset and leap seconds from the system tz database.
leapsectz right/UTC

# Specify directory for log files.
logdir /var/log/chrony

# Select which information is logged.
#log measurements statistics tracking
# Per : Set port 0 in /etc/chrony.conf
port 0
# Per : Set cmdport 0 in /etc/chrony.conf
cmdport 0
server 127.0.0.1 iburst

# Use public servers from the pool.ntp.org project.
# Please consider joining the pool (https://www.pool.ntp.org/join.html).
poolmaxpoll 10

# Use NTP servers from DHCP.
sourcedir /run/chrony-dhcp

# Record the rate at which the system clock gains/losses time.
driftfile /var/lib/chrony/drift

# Allow the system clock to be stepped in the first three updates
# if its offset is larger than 1 second.
makestep 1.0 3

# Enable kernel synchronization of the real-time clock (RTC).
rtcsync

# Enable hardware timestamping on all interfaces that support it.
#hwtimestamp *

# Increase the minimum number of selectable sources required to adjust
# the system clock.
#minsources 2

# Allow NTP client access from local network.
#allow 192.168.0.0/16

# Serve time even if not synchronized to a time source.
#local stratum 10

# Require authentication (nts or key option) for all NTP sources.
#authselectmode require

# Specify file containing keys for NTP authentication.
keyfile /etc/chrony.keys

# Save NTS keys and cookies.
ntsdumpdir /var/lib/chrony

# Insert/delete leap seconds by slewing instead of stepping.
#leapsecmode slew

# Get TAI-UTC offset and leap seconds from the system tz database.
leapsectz right/UTC

# Specify directory for log files.
logdir /var/log/chrony

# Select which information is logged.
#log measurements statistics tracking
# Per : Set port 0 in /etc/chrony.conf
port 0
# Per : Set cmdport 0 in /etc/chrony.conf
cmdport 0
server 127.0.0.1 iburst

llm_verdict.message:
It appears that the provided commands did not successfully resolve the Openscap finding "Configure Time Service Maxpoll Interval". 

The command `sed -i 's/\\(server\\|pool\\|peer\\).*maxpoll [0-9,-][0-9]*\\(.*\\)$/\\1maxpoll 10\\2/g' /etc/chrony.conf` failed to properly configure the maxpoll interval in the `/etc/chrony.conf` file.

Further investigation using `journalctl -xeu chronyd.service` shows that chronyd failed to start due to an invalid directive in the `/etc/chrony.conf` file.

To resolve this issue, it may be necessary to manually edit the `/etc/chrony.conf` file to ensure that the maxpoll interval is correctly configured for each server, pool, or peer entry. 

After making the necessary changes, it's essential to restart the chronyd service and verify that the configuration is correct using `grep maxpoll /etc/chrony.conf` and `systemctl status chronyd.service`. 

Finally, re-running the scan will confirm whether the vulnerability has been successfully resolved.
